entry.title: Re: How to set up SAP Cloud ALM Alerts for inaccessible URLs
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: How to set up SAP Cloud ALM Alerts for inaccessible URLs
entry.content: I would like to correct something: in fact, Synthetic User Monitoring do offer alert and email functionality. This is described in the pointed "Setup & Configuration" documentation.

entry.title: Re: Update dimension data in sac analytic application usi...
entry.content:
Hello @barani2 

as far as what I understand from your first cryptic sentence... I believe one of the two links might help you:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/80c9604348b9465da8098492811fd2ee.html 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/68446c8a1b89457faff1aacd0120cc01.html 
If you want to see a real life example of master data creation in planning, I would recommend you look for the Webinars about SAC Workforce Planning... and you can also install the SAC content, how SAP created the analytic application with scripts, where you can plan new employees.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP ISU - Meter to Cash
entry.content: Quite an Insight! Thank you for the information. #ISU #Utilities

entry.title: Re: Combining Responses from HTTP adapter in CPI
entry.content: Got the resolution. Closing .

entry.title: Re: $session.user in CDS for Analysis for Office
entry.content:
I have solved this by taking into consideration that parameter needs to be used in the query in order to be visible. Below is the code which makes system user parameter hidden from the prompts, but visible in the Analysis Design->Variables list: 

@Consumption.hidden: true

@EndUserText.label: 'sap user'

@Environment.systemField: #USER

P_SessionUser                : syuname,

   ….

@Consumption.filter: {selectionType: #SINGLE, multipleSelections: false, mandatory: false, hidden: true }

@Consumption.derivation: { lookupEntity: 'I_SYSTEMUSER', resultElement: 'UserID',

binding: [{ targetElement: 'UserID', type : #PARAMETER, value: 'P_SessionUser' }] }

UserID                           as SessionUser,

 

Alina

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content: Very excited to participate and learn a lot 😀 😎

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Outcome of the Q3/2024 Review
entry.content:
Dear Customers and Partners, 

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, we would like to provide an update on the progress made regarding your requests submitted through the Customer Influence portal for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
In our ongoing commitment to improving our product with and for you, we have prioritized qualified requests while still giving due consideration to requests that did not meet the voting threshold, depending on strategic fit and capacity.
 
We are happy to announce that we have provided 270+ deliveries for your improvement requests since the last review cycle in June 2024. Please find below some highlights and progress made on other requests.
 
Q3/2024 Review Outcomes

For spotlights on requests addressed throughout the last review cycle, kindly check out the summary blog post for each SAP Continuous Influence session related to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

SessionReview Summary
SAP Central Business Configuration for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Asset ManagementQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, CDS View Extraction Enablement See request status list
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Data Management and Data MigrationQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Enterprise Portfolio and Project ManagementQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, FinanceQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Private Edition, Environment, Health and SafetyQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Private Edition,
Group Reporting		Q3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, LocalizationQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ManufacturingQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Master Data MaintenanceQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services AutomationQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SalesQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ServiceQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and ProcurementQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Supply Chain LogisticsQ3/2024
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross-TopicsQ3/2024

Thank you for your continuous feedback!

entry.title: Re: Replication of HANA Views from on prem HANA to datasp...
entry.content: Hi Joe, as far as I know, filtering is not supported for SDA at all. Filters can be added to ABAP connection remote tables however. You can create a CDS as part of the VDM. Cheers. Martin

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP: Navigating Vulnerability and Patch Management in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services –
entry.content:
Hello @aarthi_kannan,

Thank you for the informative blog post on the Vulnerability testing. 

Couple of questions: 

  1. Can you please share more details on the vulnerability scanning pefromed by SAP in the systems hosted in RISE with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud?
  2. What type of vulnerability scanning is performed by SAP?  Active, Passive or both?  Can you please share available links to read more on this topic?

Thanks in advance and best regards,

Shaji

entry.title: Re: Unable to create SAP HANA Cloud DB instance in HANA C...
entry.content:
According to the error message there is already a HANA Cloud DB instance in your trial account and it is not possible to create another one.
 A HANA DB used to be created automatically in a trial account. Are you sure there is no other HANA Cloud DB instance yet?
If there is already a HANA DB, you may have to delete this one if you want to follow the tutorial step by step.

Regards, 
  Alexander

entry.content:
We had very exciting week with three wonderful SAP Community events – two Code Jams and a Meetup. I believe, there will be much more similar events in Georgia, may be not only in Tbilisi, but in other cities as well. And more SAP professional will get to know each other. We all are part of a big SAP family 😊

you can find the photos here:

https://we.tl/t-YNhvdB3cUw

https://we.tl/t-4UUCeftMoo

Thanks a lot for participation, and let's grow SAP Community in Georgia together 🙂

Thanks for your blog

Canb you explain this sentence?  because for me it's the same with the same code transaction like rsplan

Thanks

Bastien

Bastien

Dear @David_Chaviano 

Hope this message finds you well.

My name is Asma Kizer, and I am from Pakistan. I have recently Complete my learning journey in the SAP field, Please review the attached screenshot of my progress.

As I am new to the SAP field, I am eager to advance my career with a Global Certification.

Thank you for your time and assistance. 

Best regards.

entry.title: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cloud (Public) using SAP advanced SQL migration
entry.content:
Hi Javier, I need to migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to SAP HANA Cloud (Public). I was following the blog written by you. However, I got stuck there. I have tried two methods:

  1. Accessing the ASE database on local machine via VPN. I am able to access the ASE database with the given details IP is 198.162.2.105 and port is 4901 via interactive SQL. However, I was not able to extract the data via SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool as gateway was not open at the server.
  1. Second option is, I have got the direct access to the server where ASE(Sybase) database is present. I have opened the gateway there too. However, gateway host name is 198.162.2.110 /3000. Here, I could successfully open the gateway but not able to reach to the Sybase.

entry.title: Re: Sy-Batch is not setting in Background Job
entry.content: Please don't post an answer for comments, additional details. Answer is reserved to propose a solution. Instead, use Replies > Comment (on the question or on a proposed answer).

entry.title: Re: Smart Data Streaming - KAFKA Adapter - Importing JSON...
entry.content:
Hello, @RobertWaywell   For Testing my kafka adapter , I have created Project with 2  kafka input adapters. 1st one to fetch non-array json value from input adapter( Kafka_JSON_struct_input ) , 2nd one array json value from input adapter ( Kafka_JSON_array_input ). Both Input adapters connected to respective output adapters which connects them to respective HANA tables.

My Projects looks like this. 

 

When I run my project, I don't see any error in both input adapters. Both are in CONTINUOS state. 

My non-array json adapter is working fine. I'm able to fetch value from adapter and capture in input stream. My problem is while fetching json array value.
I'm not able to fetch array values into input stream.

my sample array json structure. I want to fetch from array billableitems[]. 

This is how I mapped jsonColsMappingList & jsonRootpath. 

jsonColsMappingList = 'itemid,entitlementid,purchaseauthorizationid,itembillingstate'.  /* List of column names separated by comma */
jsonRootpath = 'value.value.content.businessevent.billingitems[0]'.  /* for testing wanted to fetch 1st line from array */  

ccl file attached. 

I'm not able to figure out why I am not able to fetch  array values.  Is there something I need to add some additional parameter in input parameter ?  can you please suggest. Thanks .

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

 I hope this message finds you well.

My name is alizy Nazeer, and I am from Dubai. I have recently Done my SAP learning journey.

You can check in below attached picture.Please share Free Certification code 

Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: Re: Sy-Batch is not setting in Background Job
entry.content: At the very beginning of the job, sy-batch is always 'X' (if not, contact SAP support). The ABAP code may change it but it's highly discouraged (NEVER DO THAT). Add a Watchpoint to stop at the ABAP line of code where its value is changed.

entry.title: Re: OCI -E Procurement – A Beginners Document
entry.content:
To answer the questions above, the mapping from Catalog (HTML) to SAP values is maintained in two different places, depending on whether you use the catalog in PM or MM. 

For PM, it's like this blog describes: SPRO -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Orders -> Interface for Procurement Using Catalogs (OCI) -> Convert HTML fields to SAP fields.

For MM, the mapping happens via BAdI IF_ME_CATALOG_INTERFACE. Select the appropriate method, such as MAP_CAT_VALUES to set up the mapping. Consult with your ABAPer to fulfill your requirements. Custom fields in the PR/PO can be mapped via BAdI ME_CATALOG_INTERFACE_CUST.

See also 2448042 - Mapping additional fields from a punchout catalog to ECC - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content:
@FreekKeijzer very nice blog, thanks! fun reading on Friday afternoon :). 

I came across #3 cross-system data integration a lot of times, where BW was used as EAI as well as #2 data snapshots which kind of solves the #1 historical data issue. 

But the embedded analytics being included into the S/4 license as well as no/low-code development makes it a very interesting option vs classic BW.

#5 number of users as Martin Kreitlein pointed out is noteworthy. Some clients do provide reporting possibility to external partners (food processing during yield-time f.e.) where the number of users drastically rises what will lead to S/4 performance degradation. @MKreitlein

entry.title: Re: RAP action deep result: Field not found in the data c...
entry.content: thanks for the input, I checked your error msg again, it mentioned the field(Field __VALUE_CONTROL_VCS-REFERENCEDOCUMENT). for my case, it mentioned the nested structure. I will recreate the data element to test the issue again.

entry.title: AD104 - Next steps
entry.content:
In our Teched session AD104 you have learned about how to build CAP applications using SAP Build Code. And how to enrich this application with prebuilt functionality and service integrations by utilizing CAP Plugins.

After entering into the development environment from the SAP Build Code Lobby, we used generative AI by providing it with a textual description of our use case. Joule AI used this input and generated very useful artefacts for us:

  • A complete CDS data model
  • A CDS service definition
  • Fiori Elements UI annotations
  • Sample data

If you think of a typical 3 tier architecture everything is covered. From the CDS data model the CAP framework automatically creates the database statements covering the database layer. The CAP application itself is the server and with our CDS service definition we have defined the behavior of the server and the endpoints it provides (by default via Odata v4). And the UI annotations provide the information to render a Fiori Elements User Interface that matches our model.

So one simple command created a complete full stack application, that we can start up an try out.

And with the sample data (which even fits the business context!) we can try out the application with meaningful test data.

This is a great starting point - and we can start to enhance the application now with additional functionalities and our business logic.

One common requirement across applications is uploading attachments. On our incidents sample app, it would be very helpful to upload supporting documents. Previously, that would have been a challenge connected with a ton of custom code, maintenance and so on. Maybe a thousand lines of code to access low level APIs of an attachment service, where you need to take care of everything. Plus the additional logic - what is the maximum attachment size? How to malware scan it? How to implement the UI to upload and download attachments.

Image instead a single dependency to a reusable package and two lines of code - that includes all of the above already.

That's the power of CAP Plugins and the story behind "Calesi" - CAP level service interfaces.

I will add additional links and information to this post closer to the TechEd timeframe, stay tuned!

entry.title: BPC complexe input form
entry.content:
Hi,

For the BPC optimized or BPC embeded on premise with the last version (the choice is progess)

We want to create complex input form with the operationel planning : several comments, create the master on the fly, input the several date (start date, end date, realisation date, etc.), detail key figures with the sub input form,link with the several input form like GRDC,etc..

The BPC function are limited to answer for all needs

Do you have the other solution?

Thanks

Bastien

entry.title: Re: Performance Optimization of Calculation Views

entry.title: Re: SAC live BW Query variable not applying from URL
entry.content: Ok Finally...I could fill out the values in the prompt with this combination: https://TENANT.cloud.sap/sap/fpa/ui/app.html#/story2&/s2/ID_STORY/?v02Val=VALUE_VARIABLE2&v02Par=ID_VARIABLE1&v02Model=ID_MODEL2&v01Val=VALUE_VARIABLE1&v01Par=ID_VARIABLE2&v01Model= ID_MODEL1&mode=view Two models with two mandatory variables. I guess that with mandatory variables It is not possible to skip the initial prompt screen once the values ​​have been reported. the user must click on set the values. Thanks Lothar Thanks for answering that it is possible.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create SAP HANA Cloud DB instance in HANA C...
entry.content: Yes, I don't have any HANA Cloud DB instance yet. The first time I created, the error was "unable to connect", afterwards this error kept coming. I checked my subaccount and cloud foundry space and there was none. I tried doing it in my other subaccount using another email and it I managed to create the instance. Probably I will use that account for now.

entry.title: Multiplying Standard Values of the Work Center Formula While Calculating Standard Cost Estimate
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
We are using the S4/HANA Public Cloud Edition in our system, and while calculating standard cost estimates, the system multiplies the quantities of the standard values of the activity's work center formula. However, we expect it to only multiply the variable values of the formula. Below is the routing for the material and the master data for the work center formula.

Standard Values of the Material's Routing:

For 1 kg of material, we schedule 4 minutes for setup time (Standard Value), 10 minutes for machine time, and 15 minutes for labor time. This means our calculation for a 2.5 kg unit of material should consist of 4 minutes for setup (Standard Value), 25 minutes for machine time, and 37.5 minutes for labor time.

Work Center Formula:

Based on our formula and the routing, the quantity calculation for the cost estimate for a 2.5 kg unit of material should be as follows:
4 minutes (Setup time) + [37.5 (Labor Time) x 1 (Operation Quantity) / 1 (Base Quantity) / 1 (Operation Splits)] = 41.5 minutes


Here is the system's calculation :

(4 minutes Setup time x 2.5 = 10 minutes, and 15 minutes x 2.5 = 37.5 minutes)
The system divides the calculation based on the standard and variable values of the formula and multiplies both values, while we expect it to only multiply the variable values of the formula.

Additionally, for make-to-order materials, the system calculates the standard cost estimates for the Sales Document correctly. However, we couldn't find any different options for the material cost calculation variant.

entry.title: Changing Order Code Length in SAP Cloud Commerce
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m new to SAP and have a question regarding the project.properties file in SAP Cloud Commerce. Currently, the order code is defined like this:

keygen.order.code.name=order_code
keygen.order.code.digits=8
keygen.order.code.start=00000000
keygen.order.code.type=numeric
keygen.order.code.template=$

I understand that project.properties is a read-only file. Is there a way to change the keygen.order.code.digits value to modify the length of the orderCode? If so, what steps should I take to implement this change?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks!

Sayuj

entry.title: Guided buying validation policy- restrict supplier to multiple commodities
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm attempting to create a validation policy that would restrict a supplier to a set of commodity codes. I've been able to create a restriction to a single code with no problem, however when attempting to set this up for multiple codes using the 'or' function it's not working and blocking all codes. I've attempted single line with the different commodities separated by | as well as separate lines for each commodity, with || (or) logical operator

i.e. if Supplier = 123456 and commodity code does not equal CommodityA or CommodityB or CommodityC, give an error to block transaction.

I've played around but cannot seem to get this to work as intended. I'm new to this role in my organization so would greatly appreciate anyone's generous guidance! Example of the attempted rule is shown below.

Many thanks,

Danny Oldfield

entry.title: Re: ODATA V4 RAP - How to retrieve messages in response
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm also trying to figure this out. If you're developing using freestyle SAPUI5 and Javascript, you could get those messages like this:

// any method that triggers POST call to backend - I assume you have it
oModel.submitBatch("batchCreate"); 
oModel.attachDataReceived(function (oEvent) {
        let oSource = oEvent.getSource();
        let aMessages = oSource.getModel().getMessagesByPath("/EntityPath");
    });

Where EntityPath is your alias connection to entity maintained in Service Definition - I assume you have it covered as well.

Although, I'm not sure whether you can receive details about your Entity Key in particular message. I think this has to be maintained manually. If someone knows how to achieve it directly, let us know.

entry.title: Re: First release of B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages and their Extensions
entry.content: • Hello Saurabh, great that you found it. Kind regards, Gunther

entry.title: DOX Premium Prompting: Best Practices & Hints
entry.content:
Document Information Extraction - Premium

With the General Availability (GA) of Document Information Extraction, premium edition (DOX Premium), users now have the power to leverage Generative AI (GenAI) for extracting entities from structured documents. This cutting-edge capability allows for processing a wide variety of document types, in almost every language. However, as with most GenAI-driven applications, the success of the extraction process heavily depends on the quality of the prompts given to the model.

This blog post explores best practices for using GenAI in document extraction, focusing on optimizing performance through effective prompt engineering, refining field descriptions, and ensuring continuous improvement. By applying these insights, you can maximize the potential of GenAI-driven document processing in your organization.

The process of iterative prompt development

When working with large language models, it's essential to understand that the first query is rarely perfect. For each field requiring extraction, we begin by writing a base prompt that captures essential information (such as position, format, or context) about the entity. Once we review the results, we often need to refine the prompt based on the initial output.

However, a word of caution: modifying the description of one field can impact the performance of other fields. Therefore, in DOX Premium, any adjustments to field descriptions should be followed by an analysis of all fields, not just those that were changed. This iterative process continues until we achieve satisfactory results.

Steps for Iterative Prompt Development:

  1. Identify the field: Decide which field or specific extraction requires adjustment.
  2. Write or refine the prompt: Create a clear, specific base description.
  3. Review the results: Analyze the output for accuracy.
  4. Perform error analysis: Identify mistakes and discrepancies.
  5. Repeat: Iterate until the desired result is achieved.

 

Best Practices 

Leverage Default Extractors

Before diving into custom prompt development, take advantage of the default extractors provided by DOX Premium. These extractors are designed to select the best possible model for any given scenario—whether it's one of our own pre-trained models or a GenAI model. Trained on hundreds of thousands of business documents, they are highly accurate for common fields and document types, making them a valuable resource for initial extraction efforts. It's beneficial to check the performance of these pre-trained models, especially when the language is supported by them.

Activating these default extractors before relying on generative AI can help you assess whether further customization is needed. In many cases, combining pre-trained models with generative AI yields optimal results for specific use cases. Here's a quick guide on how to activate the default extractors:

Clear and concise field descriptions

Using clear and concise language to describe fields as part of a prompt significantly improves extraction accuracy in DOX Premium. Effective field descriptions help the model understand the specific data to extract, reducing ambiguity and errors. This is particularly important when similar fields exist, and you want to guide the model in a specific direction.

Field descriptions can also be tailored to manipulate the output format or language. In the following example from a delivery note, we compare a basic prompt with a more guided approach that provides clearer instructions. 

 

Don'tDo
Extract the countryExtract the country from the address. Please translate the country into english.
Extract the delivery conditionsExtract the delivery conditions. It is sufficient to extract only the abbreviation of the delivery condition mentioned on the document
 Material referenceMaterial Description

Extraction beforeExtraction after

In the example, we clarified the country extraction and achieved the desired outcome of translating the country name into English. For delivery conditions, specifying that only the abbreviation should be extracted helped produce a more accurate result. The last example highlights that even semantically close descriptions can generate different outputs.
Not all business documents follow such a clear, labeled structure. For instance, many purchase orders include material numbers or codes that may not have explicit labels or indicators on the document. In these cases, understanding the entities—such as recognizing a specific format or consistent positioning on the document—becomes essential. Once this knowledge is gathered, you can improve extraction by incorporating these specifics into the field descriptions.

Schema description

DOX Premium now allows you to define schema descriptions, which serve as general instructions to the large language model (LLM) before the schema and field-specific descriptions are applied. These schema descriptions can enhance the extraction process at the document level without focusing on individual fields.

One effective approach is to role-play with the LLM by assigning it a persona. For example:
“You are an expert in extracting purchase orders from company X, which are related to products Y.”

This instruction can be followed by more general directives, such as specifying language preferences or emphasizing certain priorities. Schema descriptions can also help clarify correlations between fields. For instance, you can instruct the LLM to differentiate between the sending and receiving entities:
“Company X is always the receiver/supplier of the purchase order.”

In the screenshot example, we demonstrate a schema description being used to guide the model’s understanding of a nested structure within a delivery note. In this case, the line item table contains multiple material codes that may be linked to each other. The schema description informs the LLM to treat all nested material codes as part of a single line item:

“The material codes in the line item table can have a nested structure, meaning that subsequent codes can belong to each other. Please extract all the nested material codes as one line item.”

Extraction of nested structure with schema description

This approach helps the model accurately interpret and extract data even from complex or hierarchical document structures.

Regular Expressions

If you want to extract numbers that have a standardised format, it can help to use regular expressions for that in the field description. Regular expressions are like a smart search tool that helps you find specific patterns or sequences of text in a large amount of data. For example, if you are pretty sure that the article number on purchase orders has a structure of 'xxx.xx.xx', where x is a digit between 0 and 0, you can use the following regular expressions: \d{3}\.\d{2}\.\d{2}. As most of the DOX users (like myself) are no experts in this language, you can just use ChatGPT or other related tools to generate these expressions.

Field Names

It’s important to emphasize that field names are also part of the LLM's input, which means they need to be meaningful and clear. As demonstrated in the schema description example, the wording of field names should align with the naming conventions used in the documents being processed. If users need to utilize different field identifiers for further processing, they can do so by using the 'Label' cell in the schema.

For more practical examples of field names and other related topics, you can check out the DOX help page: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-information-extraction/document-information-extraction/extraction-using-generative-ai?locale=en-US

Summary

With DOX Premium, a prompt engineer has the flexibility to get creative and guide the model in a desired direction. Throughout the process of integrating this solution, it is crucial to align the model's behavior with the specific needs of the business. Since extracted data is often processed further in the workflow, ensuring its accuracy is essential.
Regular feedback loops between business and IT teams are invaluable for improving the service's usability and overall value. These ongoing interactions help to continuously refine prompts and schema descriptions, ensuring the solution delivers consistent and reliable results.

 

New Features

Instant Learning

The instant learning feature leverages a few-shot prompting approach to significantly enhance the AI model's understanding through real-time user feedback. In a few-shot prompting approach, the AI model is provided with several examples to help it better understand and execute a particular task. This method proves particularly effective for improving accuracy in complex tasks. For example, in sentiment classification, the model's output can be refined by supplying examples of the desired result:

 

 

This is awesome! // Negative
This is bad! // Positive
Wow that movie was rad! // Positive
What a horrible show! //

 

 

Users for now need to select the Setup Type 'auto' and exclude the default extractors to activate the feedback mechanism. Once enabled, they can review and correct the extracted values. This feedback, which functions as a few-shot prompt, helps the system learn about the document layout and the corrections. The knowledge gained is then used to improve extraction accuracy for future tasks with similar document layouts.

In the screenshots below, you can see the process in the DOX UI, where I selected a simple example of a naive address definition and made adjustments to refine the extraction for the address field:

Edit the document

Apply and confirm the corrections

Next incoming (similar) document

The last screenshot shows the updated extraction when uploading the document again with the same schema. The model now extracts the correct address from the top of the document rather than the shipping address. This change persists as long as the confirmed document remains stored in the backend, ensuring consistent extractions for similar future documents.

Data Type: List of Values

The new "List of Values" data type enables classification scenarios within DOX Premium and can significantly improve extraction performance for specific fields. This feature allows users to define expected output fields for extraction, which helps guide the large language model (LLM) in its predictions.
For instance, if a user needs to classify documents as either invoices or debit notes, they can define those values in the list. The LLM will then analyze the entire document to make an informed classification. Below is an example of how this field is set up in the DOX UI:

In this case, you can also leverage the field description and the description of the list values to provide additional instructions or hints to the LLM. Another possible application of this feature is field-level classification, such as identifying the unit of measure. Here, users can define a list of possible outputs, like Kilograms or Tons, which the model will reference during extraction. 

 

Conclusion & Next Steps 

We hope this blog has demonstrated some useful hacks that you can apply to your next DOX project or use case. We would love to hear your feedback on how these techniques are working with your specific documents, or if you have additional ideas on how to improve the extraction process.
If you're still unsure whether DOX Premium is the right solution for your document needs, please don't hesitate to reach out to me or my colleagues on the AI RIG Team. We offer dedicated support to help activate the service and enable end users to successfully implement it. Additionally, we can review your documents and conduct a small Proof of Concept (PoC) using 10-20 documents, along with a live demo of the results.

christoph.batke@sap.com

sap_ai_rig@sap.com

entry.title: Re: How to get URL parameters in SAP Screen Personas
entry.content: Thank you for the reply. This doesn't seem to work, the problem is that it comes back empty:

entry.title: Re: S/4 Product Compliance - Label Management
entry.content:
Hello Shashi,

Thank you for your interest in our new Label Management solution in SAP S/4HANA for product compliance.

Regarding your first question: "... Do we create our own compliance requirement with these specific phrases and graphics?".

The regulatory information which is shown on a label comes from data maintained for the product (Unpackaged Product) for which the label is generated and printed. This means the phrases and graphics which are shown on a label do not come from the compliance requirements for the label itself, but from the compliance requirements which are used to maintain label relevant data for a product. See e.g.:

Phrases which are linked to the compliance requirement version EU-GHS Labeling (May 2022):

Graphics which are linked to the compliance requirement version EU-GHS Labeling (May 2022):

The compliance requirement for the label itself can be more compared with a generation variant for a label in the Product Compliance Classic solution. This means it defines the criteria for the data selection (and with this the validity of the label) like e.g. the Validity Area and the criteria for data formatting like e.g. Date Format or Decimal Format.

Regarding your second question: "Is the process similar to the safety data sheet process where we perform an assessment against the assigned compliance requirements that contain phrases and labels?"

In terms of the maintenance of the regulatory data which are shown on a safety data sheet and which are shown on a label, the process is the same. In fact, where applicable, the label generation and the safety data sheet generation do access the same compliance assessment to ensure consistency between the two documents.
In terms of the management of the generated documents (safety data sheets and labels) the concepts are different. For safety data sheets there are assessments for safety data sheet management compliance requirements where the generated documents can be linked and where versions can be managed. For labels, the generated documents are managed in the Partner Solution. 

Regarding your third question: "For my benefit, would it be possible to briefly describe the end-to-end process from design of template all the way to print from a business process point of view?" 

See the following slides:
Process Overview:

Process Details:

Remark: The slide above represents the current version of the Label Field Catalog. The Label Field Catalog will be extended in the future to provide more label data fields out-of-the-box.

Example for an additional Label Scenario which allows to print labels independent from a specific business process:

Regarding your additional question: "... does this label management solution support integrated checks and automatic triggering for labels as well? Or can the label only be generated manually?"

Currently the new label management solution is not directly integrated into the business processes. As shown in the slides above, an indirect integration via the Validate and Print app is supported an allows to print labels for e.g. a Delivery.
The direct integration is planned for the future. For details regarding the future planning please refer to the SAP Road Map Explorer - Product Compliance

I hope this helps.

Best regards,
Michael Schalk (SAP)
Product Manager for Label Data Management

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

I just tried to take exam in Financial Accouting private cloud and unfortunately I failed. But luckily, I find your post. I want to try the exam one more time but I have difficulty to afford the high exam fee. Working as SAP consultant gives me opportunity to work at home but can can do the job in any country. I would be great for a mother like me because it helps me to balance between worklife and develop myself to catch up with the change of age. I also have plan to learn about SAP Sales Private cloud because I have interest in SD module.

I am looking forward to hearing from you!

entry.title: Re: Sybase IQ 16.1 OLEDB driver problem with SQL Server Integration Services / Visual Studio
entry.content: Are you getting an error message? If so what is it? If not, what makes you think the issue is specifically with the driver? For example, if you revert back to an older driver does the issue go away?

entry.title: Invoice by ship-to
entry.content:
SAP Business One (version 10)

I have a business partner with multiple ship-to addresses. Each ship-to has it's own email address for invoicing. Is it possible to configure the BP so each invoice can go to different email addresses defined by the ship-to location?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: BRF+ Decision Tables - Limit what user can see and edit
entry.content:
Unless I'm missing something - I can't see how

  • On global templates - the authorisations behind BRF+ don't seem to support things like "make sure this country can only see and edit their own data on the same decision table (eg by company code or sales area)" 
  • When you filter - you can only set 1 filter (so if you have 20,000 rows it's a bit simplistic) 
  • Getting to multiple decision tables in BRF+ (from an end user point of view), isn't the most user friendly 

Has anyone used BRF+ and hit these same issues?  

  • DSM (Data Service Management) is sometimes mentioned - but it seems new and not the answer.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: Hello @David_Chaviano , I’m excited to participate in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I recently shifted to a career as a junior SAP MM consultant after working in software development and construction engineering. I’m determined to grow my SAP expertise and work towards certification. However, I currently lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt, which makes this initiative especially valuable to me. I’m confident that my technical skills and determination will help me succeed. Looking forward to learning with the community and engaging with this program!

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - How to edit an object inside another?
entry.content:
Let me try to clarify for you.  In your first screen shot, the list page is targeting NOTICIA to display a list of all records in the entity set.  I am assuming you have an OnPress action on the Object Table to Navigate to the next screen shot.

Side note: When setting a target you use either Entity Set or Read Link but not both.

When the OnPress executes, the selected NOTICIA record from the list becomes the default binding object for the next page.  This means that when you bind values on the detail page you do not need to do another call to the odata service to get the data.  You can directly bind controls to the properties of the NOTICIA record.

In your second screen shot you have set the DesignTimeTarget for the page to the NOTICIAPERSONALIKE entity set.  The Design Time target is ONLY used by the Editor to help filter the Object Browser based to the Design time target to simplify picking properties.  

Without it the editor does not have any knowledge of what the default binding object will be when the page opens.  This property was added to make it easier for developers to define when they expect the default binding object to be when the page opens.  In your case I believe there may be a mismatch between what the default binding object is and what you have set as the Design Time Target but I could be wrong since I don't know from where and how the navigation was called.

From your CAP service there is a one to many relationship between NOTICIA and NOTICIAPERSONALIKE.  When a user first likes the NOTICIA there is no entry in NOTICIAPERSONALIKE and you can call CreateEntity targeting the NOTICIAPERSONALIKE entity set to create the entry.  However, once that entry exists you will need to call UpdateEntity and target the specific NOTICIAPERSONALIKE record to change the Like value.

In your third screenshot, the target is pointing to {@odata.readLink}/NOTICIAPERSONALIKES where I assume {@odata.readLink} points to the selected NOTICIA record.  In this case NOTICAPERSONALIKES is a navigation property to the list of all the likes for the NOTICIA and does not uniquely point to the one like for the current user.

To target the like for the current user you would either need the readLink for the existing user like or need to user query options and target the entity set.

EntitySet: NOTICIAPERSONALIKE
Query Options: $filter=NOTICIA eq '{NOTICIA}' and PERSONA = 'current user persona value'

These values are a guess.  If you can share the $metadata for NOTICIA and NOTICIAPERSONALIKE we can better identify what properties would be needed in the query options.

The error in your last screen shot is trying to tell you that the target for your UpdateEntity action is incorrect and does not uniquely point to a single record to be updated.

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: How to use 2FA for Fiori
entry.content: The question it too broad but this is not a Fiori question. What enforces 2FA is where you authenticate to. If you are using Azure AD to autheticate users, then is defined in Azure AD. You might have IAS or any other similar tool to create that connection is not Fiori where you authenticate.

entry.title: Capacity Utilization for a group
entry.content:
Hello Experts,


In TS Unconstrained RCCP we calculate Capacity Utilization for production resource at Resource-Location level. The requirement is to calculate utilization separately for different product groups. 

For example - at Resource 1 , 10 products can be produced that belong to Group 1 & 5 products can be produced that belong to Group 2. Utilization calculated for both groups at RES-LOC is 90%. We have the Capacity by product where we can see KF 'Capacity Demand of Production Resource' at RES-LOC-PROD-SRC level, but doesn't relate back to Capacity Utilization.

If the capacity utilization for Group 1 = 70% & for Group 2 = 20%, then how can this be configured? Can this be achieved? Please suggest.


Regards,

A Basu

entry.title: Gropup reporting on premise 2023/SP1 - enable the hierarchy for WBS elements
entry.content:
Hi,

With the code transnactionFINCS_ADDLFLD_SEL_U, you can manage the hierarchy in group reporting modul

You can activate the hierarchy for cost center, profit center, business area, functional aera,etc..

But you can't active the hierarchy for wbs elements , see the copy

Do you know the reason ?

Thanks

Bastien

entry.title: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
It’s the one, the only, Petoberfest! As is now tradition during Devtoberfest, we are asking you once again to post pictures of your pets - pets of any kind. All you need to do is share a picture of a furry, scaly, feathery, leafy, stuffed, etc. friend who may or may not keep you company throughout your workday.


To participate, you only need to complete the following steps.

Step 1: Take a picture of your pet in a work, coding, or general office setting (which includes work from home or alternative setups).

Step 2: Post that picture in the comments of this discussion post before EOD September 29th. If you’ve already posted the picture elsewhere, feel free to link that in the comments.

Step 3 (optional): Peruse the rest of the pet pictures posted here at your leisure and enjoy all that Petoberfest has to offer.

Be as creative or relaxed as you want to be, the only thing that’s important is to have fun.

 

As for myself, I have a cat that is obsessed with lap time, no matter what I’m doing. Here she is, purring and enjoying the free warmth.

Cheesecake once again pleased she’s on a lap

Note: You aren’t restricted to only posting during Friday, September 27th. This is open the entire first week of Devtoberfest, from September 23rd through September 29th. We will award points and badges after that time. 

Have fun and I’m looking forward to seeing everyone’s pets!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Hello @David_Chaviano 

I'm from Morocco, a woman and the first college graduate in my family, I come from a remote area where economic opportunities are limited compared to urban or more privileged regions. This makes me even more driven to learn and grow in this field to build a successful career.
I am eager to take this free certification opportunity because I believe it will enhance my skills and help me become even better at what I do.

entry.title: Re: BDC Questionnaire
entry.content: If your employer or customer has SAP entitlements you can ask to be given an S-user ID. You can then use this S-user to access SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer and as the BDC questionnaires are categorized as "Customer" assets, you will be able to access them when you log in to the site.

entry.title: Re: Quantity is not updating in Contract/outline agreemen...
entry.content:
Hello, 

The Contract with Customer is for 1000 Pcs. 

Now for one of the Sales Orders (created with reference to Contract) with quantity of 100 pcs, the Delivery and Bill Quantity will also be 100 pcs. 

In your case since the Customer received only 80 pcs, and you had to make an addition Delivery of 20 pcs, this cannot be reflected in the Contract. 

This additional Order and Delivery is due to loss of materials in Transit and cannot be reduced from Contract Quantity. 

Even after this additional Delivery of 20 pcs, the remaining Contract Quantity is 900 pcs you are supposed to Delivery 900 pcs. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: How to attach more than two SCORM 2004 packages for one item?

entry.title: Fiori Elements Overview Page using OData V4 - Why is not there yet?
entry.content:
Why is there no overview pages created using OData V4?

I came across Development of a Fiori Elements 'Overview Page' ba... - SAP Community and I saw something that has been intriguing me for some time now.

As the author mentions in the context of Overview pages:

I wasn't able to get it running with UI - V4 Odata, maybe this isnt supported yet?

This same post has a comment with a snapshot from a OpenSAP discussion from around 2021 saying that OVP do not support OData V4 entirely. You can use it for the cards but not for the FilterBar. I really wonder why and how that makes development even harder in UI5 - 2 services, 2 models, 2 mocks, and how to link them to make the filter bar work.

We are now almost in Q4/2024. Much is said about the importance and benefits of OData V4, Fiori Elements and RAP: Still there is not even a single standard app created using those according to Fiori Apps Library.

This is not mentioned in Fiori Elements Roadmap Despite having of stuff which correlates with OData V4 and tooling. 

Keep in mind that BAS supports an OData V4 service while creating a app based on the official OVP template.

I would like to create with this post a good source of truth around that and open the discussion around the importance of that. In case anyone has an update in the future that OData V4 is not fully supported for OVP, please add a reply here as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Adapter Integration
entry.content:
Hi , 

 Initially the implementation worked well but lately I am getting intermittent errors . 

  LastError           = com.sap.adapters.factory.sharepoint.exception.SharePointException: Problem during retrieving the access token for the OAuth2 client credential HTTPReceiverSharePointCreateFolder: java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method com.sap.esb.security.TrustManagerFactory.getTrustManagers() of a null object loaded from local variable 'trustManagerFactory', cause: java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method com.sap.esb.security.TrustManagerFactory.getTrustManagers() of a null object loaded from local variable 'trustManagerFactory'

Based on the error above it seems like adapter is unable to retrieve the credentials from the security materials or the adapter is unable to retrieve a token from the Microsoft token engine. 

Has anyone else experience this problem. Any suggestions would be appreciated .

Thank you .

Robert

Thanks in advance for tips!

M.

Narasimha

entry.title: Re: Error during client build SSAM 2405: Could not find c...
entry.content:
Hi,

I am running pretty much the same landscape as you have and I can build it flawlessly. Could you please check your settings in MDKProject.json? Here is an example of the relevant lines of my MDKproject.json. 

"Externals": ["@nativescript/geolocation", "extension-MapFramework", "extension-LocationService", "extension-GenericWebView", "uuid", "@nstudio/nativescript-dynatrace", "@nativescript/core/accessibility", "xml-js", "stream", "buffer", "emitter"],
"NSPlugins": ["@nativescript/geolocation", "@nstudio/nativescript-dynatrace@1.0.1"],
"NPMPlugins": ["uuid@9.0.1", "nativescript-dev-xcode", "@nativescript/hook", "xml-js", "stream", "buffer", "emitter"],
"UrlScheme": "samclient"

Beside this, please run the MDK dependency installer and the install.command, to make sure the configuration of your build folder is valid.

Hopefully this will cure your issue,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: YTD Calculation in SAC Excel Add in Using GETDATA()
entry.content:
I have solved this by having multiple data sources. DS_1 through to DS_12, using the master filter in the data source so that DS_1 = Jan, DS_2 = Jan+ Feb etc. 

Providing you do not reference the month in the Sap.Getdata formula this appears to work well, a simple lookup within the getdata formula to different data sources dependant on the date selected in your workbook works well.

entry.title: 🟡 Bugtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
New this year, for our Week 3 Fun Friday event, we are going bug hunting! What type of bugs you might ask. Well, every developer’s least favorite type of course. Coding bugs.

Open until the end of Devtoberfest, you will be able to submit how many bugs you found in one or more of the bug hunts to the appropriate tutorial. There are various languages available you can try, including JavaScript, Python, and ABAP.

The bugs will range from easy to medium with some specific language quirks to see if you know your stuff. Each language will award a unique badge and points so completing all of them could give you a leg up on the game board.

Check back at the start of Week 3 for more detail on the bug hunt.

Let the Bug Hunt Begin!

entry.title: RAP: How to use text instead of guid in value help?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have the following problem with value help in managed scenario.

In my app I want to create an object of the Model (car model) entity, which references another entity Make.

Therefore I need to pass the GUID of selected Make:

On the screen for model creation I only want to present the text value of the entry selected in value help (TOYOTA), not the key value (guid). The GUID of the car make I would like to hide from the user, but of course to pass it for object creation.

How can I achieve it?

Is there a way to make the field for Make editable and to hide the field for MakeId at the same time?

When I declare extra field for Make in the behavior definition, then I am required to create this field in the draft table, which would lead to data redundancy.

Here is the value help definition, but it only work on the MakeId field:

entry.title: SCORM Video Hosting - vBrick Rev
entry.content:
We are using iContent for our LMS content storage. Our Learning Team has expressed some frustration with how iContent handles videos (either in general, or videos that are part of SCORM packages). They would like a more "youtube-like" video hosting experience - automatic resizing, automatic changing the resolution (240p, 360p, 720p, 1080p, etc.) based on connection quality, better captioning capabilities, etc.

Our company also uses vBrick Rev for internal video hosting. Has anyone heard of (or built your own) integration between Rev and SF-LMS for hosting videos?

entry.title: Re: Pre-delivered PCC validations.
entry.content: The pre-delivered validations can typically be found within the provisioning

entry.title: Re: Pre-delivered PCC validations.
entry.content: To to the Provisioning Control Center, Find the section where delivered validations are stored; Click on the specific validations rule you wish to copy,If thee is an option to export or download, use that to get the validation in a suitable format like CSV or Excel. or manual CTRL+c Navigate to the location where you want paste the validation use Ctrl+V, thee save

entry.title: RF Enviornment create / confirm adhoc Product not confirming
entry.content:
I have set this up in many tenants with no issues. However in the RF env. in this tenant when I go to Adhoc WT Creation -> Crea/Confirm Adhoc Prod I can select all the fields, source bin/ product/ qty/ destination. When i execute I get the error 😕

NO warehouse order Designed for resouce XXX (/scmm/RF_DE123

from there if I look in sap a warehouse task is created that I have to confirm. In other tenants the task is create when i do the movemet on the scanner and confirmed and other action is needed. 

what is missing here?

@Christian_Ries

entry.title: BYD2408 Tax determination in Intercompany scenario
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

This is regarding the Tax determination logic in IC PO in cases where Ship-To is different from Bill-To, where Ship-To address is being picked for Tax determination .
Typically in most of the our IC scenarios, the Bill to = ship to and in these cases the Tax Determination rule set up is fine. But there are cases where Ship-To is different from Bill-To, and in these cases Customer address in being picked for Tax determination .

Since the client is a provider of Services, the system should always pick Bill to Address for Tax determination . Is there a provision to change this behavior in standard SAP ?

BR,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 Tax determination in Intercompany scenario
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

In your scenario, you have mentioned that Ship-To is different from Bill-To, and in these cases Customer address in being picked for Tax determination. 
The answer is Yes and it is correct. 

In purchasing, always system determines the taxes based on the destination principle. Meaning, the taxes are levied where the products or services are delivered. So when you enter customer, system picks this address as a base for tax determination. 

For transnational business transactions, the system uses a tax decision tree for both the ship-from country and the ship-to country to determine the taxation method used in the tax event for each country. The relevant decision trees are determined by the tax countries entered in the document, in particular by the countries of the supplier and the goods recipient. 
Other tax jurisdictions can be the:
-Location of the business residence of the seller who provides a service
- Location of the business residence of the buyer for whom a service is provided
-Location where the service is to be provided

The appropriate method for calculating tax is determined by a combination of tax type, tax event, and tax rate type, and by the deductibility type for the input tax of the sales volume.

Each tax code represents a unique combination of the following parameters:

In sales: 
Tax event
Tax types
Related tax rate types

In purchasing:
Tax event
Tax types
Related tax rate types
Deductibility types

Hope it clarifies. 

Best Regards,
Arun

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Integration with Weighing and Measurement Scale
entry.content:
Hi Mahesh,

were you able do integration from sap ui5 to weighing scale.

can you please provide insight to how to achieve it .

Regards,

Chaitali Desai

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Chemicals - New Launch
entry.content:
In this article I would like to provide the information about Reference Business and Solution Architecture, or “Best Practices”, proposed by SAP as a business software vendor to Chemicals and other Process Industries.

What is the SAP Enterprise Architecture

Before jumping into the industry content, I would like to explain what these “Best Practices” or the Reference Business and Solution Architecture are in general. This is set of pre-defined SAP provided content which includes:

Illustration: SAP Enterprise Architecture Scheme

  • Business process map for Levels 1, 2, 3, 4 (Level 4 relates to business activities)
  • Business process variances for different scenarios (example: Make to Stock, Make to Order, and others)
  • Business Capabilities suggested to use for each of business activity.
  • SAP Solution Capabilities supporting respective Business Capabilities
  • SAP Solutions providing respective Capabilities.

Best Practices for Chemicals content provided by SAP

Illustration: Chemicals Process Map L1 from SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

The SAP provided content for Chemical and Process Industries consists of processes classified by enterprise domains. (please use this link to access Signavio environment with The Chemicals content)

Products and Services Domain:

  • Idea to Market, I2M

Customers & Sales Domain

  • Lead to Cash, L2C

Supply Domain

  • Source to Pay, S2P
  • Plan to Fulfill, P2F

Corporate Governance Domain

  • Recruit to Retire, R2R
  • Acquire to Decommission, A2D
  • Governance and Sustainability, GOV
  • Finance, FIN

Each domain consists of business process variances relevant for Chemical and Process Industries. For example, for the “Lead to Cash” you can find variances like

  • Lead to Cash for Make to Order (process industry)
  • Lead to Cash for Sell from Stock
  • Lead to Cash for Commodities
  • Waste Collection Service
  • … and others

Illustration: Lead to Cash - End to End process variances

Illustration: Lead to Cash for Make to Order (process industry)

On the illustration above you can see the L2 process details on example of "Lead to Cash for Make to Order (process industry)" process variance. You may also drill down to Level 3, Level 4 (capabilities) and further. Later on I will explain this in details. Also please note that same level of details we have prepared and published for all E2E business processes and domains.

Two types of deployment

Today SAP proposes two deployment strategies for Solution Architecture for our respective customers in Chemicals and Process Industries:

  • Hybrid, which is based on RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition (link)
  • and Cloud, which is based GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (link)

These deployment strategies are being developed and positioned by SAP for different customer segments. Either you are global multinational business with different product divisions, or strong regional company, or medium or even small chemical fabric with grow ambitions – you will find and leverage the solution strategy and related reference content which fits you best. You can find more information about on our site sap.com.

From the Enterprise Architecture point of view, we have also developed and published two contents chapters both for Solution Capabilities and for Respective SAP Solutions. So, you can find and leverage Best Practices content for each type of deployment – depending on your Business Strategy and Intention to Cloud Services.

SAP Solutions to support the transformation 

Illustration: SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX

We published our Best Practices for Chemicals and Process Industries Content on our tools:

  • SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, which is designed for business process development and collaboration related tasks (SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation Suite)
  • and SAP LeanIX, which is designed to manage enterprise architecture, including capabilities map, solution architecture and landscape, and design of digital transformation roadmap with multi stages and phases (SAP LeanIX | Enterprise Architecture Transformation)

So you can use these tool and the reference industry content as the ready-to-use building blocks to define your own transformation roadmap which will serve best for your company.

Conclusion

Being a world leader in business software we are rooted in Chemical Industry. More than 40 years we are taking care about our customers’ success. We are committed to continues innovations for the industries, and our customers.

Choose SAP and run your digital transformation with SAP Best Practices for Chemical and Process Industries to reach speed, gain business outcomes, Grow and Rise with SAP. Want to know more? Please visit our page on sap.com

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions about.

Sergey Nozhenko, SAP Industry Business Unit for Chemicals

entry.title: Re: Batch Derivation in Process Manufacturing
entry.content:
I have a related question.  I have Sender Rules for 2 characteristics to be sent on Goods Receipt and a
Receiver rule set up to receive both characteristics. Both receiver records are set to Warning only. 

If the receiving material is produced, but only one of the two materials were issued and consumed in the prep.  This results in only 1 characteristic to be sent.  Neither characteristic is being recorded in the receiving batch.  Is that because the receiving record needs to have both sending records sent and if they are not, then neither derivation completes?

entry.title: Re: Variance (Q/Q and Y/Y) in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you Umasaral! That helps.

Can you pls send me a help article on how to create relative references to cells? I tried searching SAC Help documentation and could not find one 😞

Thank you again for all your help!

regards

Nagarajan

entry.title: Re: Update OITM
entry.content: No, Everything was great before I wrote update query. Right now I can't add documents (for example item, warehouse, itemgroups, invoice, order, etc). I have a problem with SAP hana database. I think I have changed structure of database OITM table.

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Geeks
entry.content:
Things surfaced while downsizing:

 I am getting ready to apply this patch. Heh. @oliver

entry.title: Re: How to access application with app/router
entry.content:
This is my xs-app.json

{
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "logout": {
    "logoutEndpoint": "/app-logout",
    "logoutPage": "/"
  },
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/app/(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "localDir": ".",
      "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/appconfig/",
      "localDir": ".",
      "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/user-api(.*)",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "sap-approuter-userapi"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "csrfProtection": true,
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

entry.title: Re: DBMS gateway connectivity issue
entry.content: Can you confirm if you have openned the gateway on your local machine or the database server?

entry.title: Download File from Solution Documentation 7.2 in PDF Format
entry.content: I have a validation requirement to download files from Solution Documentation in a PDF format.  What is the best way to download a file from Solution Documentation in Solution Manager 7.2 in the PDF format?  The current download process or print content process only allows for downloading as an MS Word file.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Apps + Allow Anonymous Access
entry.content:
Hi! Hope you're doing well! 😀

We’re facing a challenge similar to what you described. Our goal is to bypass the BTP login prompt and land directly on our app or on our custom login page. We saw the “API Key only” option in the security settings of mobile services, but we’re unsure how to implement it.

Did you find a way to allow users to access the app without BTP credentials, but still authenticate for specific functionalities later? Additionally, we’re unclear about how to deploy the app to the app stores and whether users will need to use the “Mobile Services” app, or if they can download it directly from the store like any regular app.

I’d really appreciate any guidance or information you can share based on your experience.

Thanks so much in advance!

Best regards, Sol

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Apps + Allow Anonymous Access
entry.content: Hi Bill, I hope you're doing well! We are currently working on a project where we are using OData V4 services configured in SAP BTP MDK, and we're encountering a challenge similar to what itsumerfarooq described. Our goal is to bypass the BTP login prompt and directly land on either the app or our custom login page. We've come across the “API Key only” option in the security settings of Mobile Services but are unsure how to properly implement it. Have you found any way to allow users to access the app without BTP credentials upfront but still authenticate for specific functionalities when needed? Also, we're unsure whether we can deploy the app directly to the app stores and let users download it like a regular app, or if they’ll still need to access it via the “Mobile Services” app. I'd appreciate any guidance or insights you could offer based on your experience. Thanks so much in advance! Best regards, Sol

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – Setting SOAP Fault Messages for a SOAP based Sender Channel
entry.content:
Dear @joerg_kessler ,

thanks for your blog.

In your XiFault in SXMB_MONI I can see a MainDocument payload.
Could you please tell how to achieve this with your code sample?

Thanks and kr,
Jo

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Dear Digital Skills Initiative Team,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to share that I have successfully completed the learning journey “Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform” and “Developing with SAP Integration Suite” through SAP Learning. I am currently pursuing an MSc Information Systems Management.

I am from Pakistan, a country currently facing economic challenges, and I am the sole earner for my family. I am providing SAP support in my company for the past four years; this opportunity has been incredibly valuable for me. It will help me not only promote in my company but also advance my career in SAP

The Digital Skills Initiative, including access to the SAP Learning Hub and the certification attempt initiative, has been instrumental in helping me advance my career in SAP. I am eager to continue learning about emerging technologies, especially as SAP continues to lead in ERP with its focus on the Business Technology Platform (BTP) and innovations like Joule AI

Thank you for providing such a supportive platform for growth. I look forward to any future opportunities to enhance my skills and knowledge.

Yours Sincerely,

Muhammad Zohaib

sgmzohaib

entry.title: Re: S/4 Product Compliance - Label Management
entry.content:
Hello

regarding:

2. design label template using external partner solution (this replaces the old classic WWI approach). send template back to S/4 product compliance

My understanding here is different. The "template" will not come back to SAP.

Any data as maintained (and needed for the process etc.) will be shared to "third party" tool. Here (as you have stated correct) the "layout" is defined. As part of layout a "data mapping" is done (so the data coming from SAP is mapped to the structures of the "thirs party solution"

May be SAP can confirm this understanding

Thanks.

C.B.

entry.title: Re: S/4 Product Compliance - Label Management
entry.content: MANY THANKS FOR THIS DETAIL EXPLANATION OF CURRENT STATUS OF THE SOLUTION !

entry.title: Re: SAP PI Configuration for JWT Authentication to DocuSign APIs
entry.content:
Hello Saurabh,

thank you for a very helpful blog. I would like to ask you, whether you have any experience with firebase google API service? If yes, could you please check with me one configuration. I did what you mentioned above, but I am still getting the same error - Could not retrieve oAuth Token.

I have these docu

https://firebase.google.com/docs/auth/admin/verify-id-tokens#retrieve_id_tokens_on_clients

and this is my configuration:

Are you able to see any problem?

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Public Cloud Edition / Recieve E-Invoices
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

I apologize I am not familiar with X-Rechnung, however, this looks promising:

Processing Electronic Customer Invoices and Submitting Them Using E-Mail

For receiving the supplier invoice, if you have it electronically on a share, or local file, you can utilize the app Upload Supplier Invoice in S/4HANA Cloud public edition (typically it expects a PDF but you can investigate support for XML) (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/index.html#/detail/Apps('F2452')/S33)

Help link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/210c1c5845359344e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

I did notice the Fiori app library references this app is associated with 1LE which is no longer available to new customers. I don't believe the app relies on 1LE however.

More about Invoice Processing in S/4HANA Cloud: https://solutionportfolio.net.sap/industry/CROSS/object/6EAE8B27FCC11ED892E92460814BA0CC

 

Please check 4N6 Invoice Processing with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (4N6)(https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2408/SolP/4N6?region=DE)

For import via email, CIM has a RPA bot that can bring in the supplier invoice from an email.

CIM supports the following document types: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-information-extraction/document-information-extraction/supported-document-types-and-file-formats?locale=en-US

I hope this helps

Thank you
Jerry
